items.title: Prefer typed columnar storage for large tables
items.avoid: Repeated CSV reads of multi-GB files in production pipelines.
items.prefer: Parquet (or Feather) with explicit column lists at read time.
items.why: Columnar formats reduce I/O and preserve dtypes; CSV parsing is CPU-heavy and schema-less.
items.example: `pd.read_parquet("train.parquet", columns=["f1", "f2", "label"])` instead of `read_csv` on a 40 GB export.

items.title: Specify dtypes for identifiers and labels
items.avoid: Default inference for ID, zip, and SKU columns.
items.why: Numeric-looking IDs lose leading zeros and waste memory as float64.
items.example: Reload `orders.csv` with `order_id` as string so "00042" stays exact.

items.title: Audit memory before downstream joins
items.avoid: Chaining merges on a DataFrame you have not sized.
items.why: Object and float64 columns dominate RAM; early fixes avoid OOM during join explosions.
items.example: Discovering a single text column uses 2 GB prompts casting to category or dropping it before merge.

items.title: Plan split boundaries at load time for ordered data
items.avoid: Shuffling or random sampling immediately after loading time-series or session data.
items.prefer: Sort by time or session key, document split indices, then apply train-only fits.
items.why: Random shuffles at load destroy temporal structure and invite leakage when stats are computed globally.
items.example: After `read_parquet`, `df.sort_values("timestamp")` then `train = df.iloc[:split_idx]` for a rolling validation window.
